types.name: IncludeEnum
types.description:
Specify additional output data to include in the model response. Currently supported values are:
- `web_search_call.results`: Include the search results of the web search tool call.
- `web_search_call.action.sources`: Include the sources of the web search tool call.
- `code_interpreter_call.outputs`: Includes the outputs of python code execution in code interpreter tool call items.
- `computer_call_output.output.image_url`: Include image urls from the computer call output.
- `file_search_call.results`: Include the search results of the file search tool call.
- `message.input_image.image_url`: Include image urls from the input message.
- `message.output_text.logprobs`: Include logprobs with assistant messages.
- `reasoning.encrypted_content`: Includes an encrypted version of reasoning tokens in reasoning item outputs. This enables reasoning items to be used in multi-turn conversations when using the Responses API statelessly (like when the `store` parameter is set to `false`, or when an organization is enrolled in the zero data retention program).

fields.name: items
fields.description: The items to add to the conversation. You may add up to 20 items at a time.

types.name: EasyInputMessage

fields.name: role
fields.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

fields.name: content
fields.description:
Text, image, or audio input to the model, used to generate a response.
Can also contain previous assistant responses.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the message input. Always `message`.

types.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role. Messages with the
`assistant` role are presumed to have been generated by the model in previous
interactions.

types.name: EasyInputMessageRole
types.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

types.name: EasyInputMessageContent
types.description:
Text, image, or audio input to the model, used to generate a response.
Can also contain previous assistant responses.

fields.name: type

fields.name: text

types.name: InputImageContent

fields.name: type

types.description: An image input to the model. Learn about [image inputs](/docs/guides/vision).

types.name: InputFileContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_file`.

fields.name: filename

fields.name: file_url
fields.description: The URL of the file to be sent to the model.

types.description: A file input to the model.

types.name: MessagePhase
types.description:
Labels an `assistant` message as intermediate commentary (`commentary`) or the final answer (`final_answer`).
For models like `gpt-5.3-codex` and beyond, when sending follow-up requests, preserve and resend
phase on all assistant messages — dropping it can degrade performance. Not used for user messages.

`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

types.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role.

fields.description: The score threshold for the file search, a number between 0 and 1. Numbers closer to 1 will attempt to return only the most relevant results, but may return fewer results.

fields.description: The weight of the embedding in the reciprocal ranking fusion.

fields.name: text_weight
fields.description: The weight of the text in the reciprocal ranking fusion.

types.name: ComparisonFilter

fields.name: type
fields.description:
Specifies the comparison operator: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `in`, `nin`.
- `eq`: equals
- `ne`: not equal
- `gt`: greater than
- `gte`: greater than or equal
- `lt`: less than
- `lte`: less than or equal
- `in`: in
- `nin`: not in

fields.name: key
fields.description: The key to compare against the value.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value to compare against the attribute key; supports string, number, or boolean types.

types.description: A filter used to compare a specified attribute key to a given value using a defined comparison operation.

types.name: ComparisonFilterType
types.description:
Specifies the comparison operator: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `in`, `nin`.
- `eq`: equals
- `ne`: not equal
- `gt`: greater than
- `gte`: greater than or equal
- `lt`: less than
- `lte`: less than or equal
- `in`: in
- `nin`: not in

types.name: ComparisonFilterValue
types.description: The value to compare against the attribute key; supports string, number, or boolean types.

types.name: CompoundFilter

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of operation: `and` or `or`.

fields.name: filters
fields.description: Array of filters to combine. Items can be `ComparisonFilter` or `CompoundFilter`.

types.description: Combine multiple filters using `and` or `or`.

types.name: CompoundFilterType
types.description: Type of operation: `and` or `or`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the computer tool. Always `computer`.

types.description: A tool that controls a virtual computer. Learn more about the [computer tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-computer-use).

fields.name: display_width

fields.name: display_height

types.description: A tool that controls a virtual computer. Learn more about the [computer tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-computer-use).

types.name: WebSearchTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search` or `web_search_2025_08_26`.

fields.name: search_context_size
fields.description: High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the default.

types.description:
Search the Internet for sources related to the prompt. Learn more about the
[web search tool](/docs/guides/tools-web-search).

types.name: WebSearchToolType
types.description: The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search` or `web_search_2025_08_26`.

types.name: WebSearchToolFilters
types.description: Filters for the search.

types.name: WebSearchApproximateLocation

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of location approximation. Always `approximate`.

types.description: The approximate location of the user.

types.name: WebSearchToolSearchContextSize
types.description: High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the default.

types.name: McpTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the MCP tool. Always `mcp`.

fields.name: server_label
fields.description: A label for this MCP server, used to identify it in tool calls.

fields.name: server_url
fields.description:
The URL for the MCP server. One of `server_url` or `connector_id` must be
provided.

fields.name: connector_id
fields.description:
Identifier for service connectors, like those available in ChatGPT. One of
`server_url` or `connector_id` must be provided. Learn more about service
connectors [here](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp#connectors).

Currently supported `connector_id` values are:

- Dropbox: `connector_dropbox`
- Gmail: `connector_gmail`
- Google Calendar: `connector_googlecalendar`
- Google Drive: `connector_googledrive`
- Microsoft Teams: `connector_microsoftteams`
- Outlook Calendar: `connector_outlookcalendar`
- Outlook Email: `connector_outlookemail`
- SharePoint: `connector_sharepoint`

fields.name: authorization
fields.description:
An OAuth access token that can be used with a remote MCP server, either
with a custom MCP server URL or a service connector. Your application
must handle the OAuth authorization flow and provide the token here.

fields.name: server_description
fields.description: Optional description of the MCP server, used to provide more context.

fields.name: defer_loading
fields.description: Whether this MCP tool is deferred and discovered via tool search.

types.description:
Give the model access to additional tools via remote Model Context Protocol
(MCP) servers. [Learn more about MCP](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp).

types.name: McpToolConnectorId
types.description:
Identifier for service connectors, like those available in ChatGPT. One of
`server_url` or `connector_id` must be provided. Learn more about service
connectors [here](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp#connectors).

Currently supported `connector_id` values are:

- Dropbox: `connector_dropbox`
- Gmail: `connector_gmail`
- Google Calendar: `connector_googlecalendar`
- Google Drive: `connector_googledrive`
- Microsoft Teams: `connector_microsoftteams`
- Outlook Calendar: `connector_outlookcalendar`
- Outlook Email: `connector_outlookemail`
- SharePoint: `connector_sharepoint`

types.name: McpToolAllowedTools
types.description: List of allowed tool names or a filter object.

types.name: McpToolFilter

fields.name: tool_names
fields.description: List of allowed tool names.

fields.name: read_only
fields.description:
Indicates whether or not a tool modifies data or is read-only. If an
MCP server is [annotated with `readOnlyHint`](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/schema#toolannotations-readonlyhint),
it will match this filter.

types.description: A filter object to specify which tools are allowed.

types.name: McpToolRequireApproval
types.description: Specify which of the MCP server's tools require approval.

types.name: McpToolRequireApprovalMcpToolApprovalFilter
types.description:
Specify which of the MCP server's tools require approval. Can be
`always`, `never`, or a filter object associated with tools
that require approval.

types.name: McpToolRequireApprovalMcpToolApprovalSetting
types.description:
Specify a single approval policy for all tools. One of `always` or
`never`. When set to `always`, all tools will require approval. When
set to `never`, all tools will not require approval.

types.name: CodeInterpreterTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the code interpreter tool. Always `code_interpreter`.

fields.name: container
fields.description:
The code interpreter container. Can be a container ID or an object that
specifies uploaded file IDs to make available to your code, along with an
optional `memory_limit` setting.

types.description: A tool that runs Python code to help generate a response to a prompt.

types.name: CodeInterpreterToolContainer
types.description:
The code interpreter container. Can be a container ID or an object that
specifies uploaded file IDs to make available to your code, along with an
optional `memory_limit` setting.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Always `auto`.

fields.name: file_ids
fields.description: An optional list of uploaded files to make available to your code.

fields.name: network_policy
fields.description: Network access policy for the container.

types.description: Configuration for a code interpreter container. Optionally specify the IDs of the files to run the code on.

types.name: AutoCodeInterpreterToolParamNetworkPolicy
types.description: Network access policy for the container.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Disable outbound network access. Always `disabled`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Allow outbound network access only to specified domains. Always `allowlist`.

fields.name: allowed_domains
fields.description: A list of allowed domains when type is `allowlist`.

fields.name: domain_secrets
fields.description: Optional domain-scoped secrets for allowlisted domains.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: The domain associated with the secret.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the secret to inject for the domain.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The secret value to inject for the domain.

types.name: ImageGenTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the image generation tool. Always `image_generation`.

fields.name: quality
fields.description:
The quality of the generated image. One of `low`, `medium`, `high`,
or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: size
fields.description: The size of the generated images. For `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-2026-04-21`, arbitrary resolutions are supported as `WIDTHxHEIGHT` strings, for example `1536x864`. Width and height must both be divisible by 16 and the requested aspect ratio must be between 1:3 and 3:1. Resolutions above `2560x1440` are experimental, and the maximum supported resolution is `3840x2160`. The requested size must also satisfy the model's current pixel and edge limits. The standard sizes `1024x1024`, `1536x1024`, and `1024x1536` are supported by the GPT image models; `auto` is supported for models that allow automatic sizing. For `dall-e-2`, use one of `256x256`, `512x512`, or `1024x1024`. For `dall-e-3`, use one of `1024x1024`, `1792x1024`, or `1024x1792`.

fields.name: output_format
fields.description:
The output format of the generated image. One of `png`, `webp`, or
`jpeg`. Default: `png`.

fields.name: output_compression
fields.description: Compression level for the output image. Default: 100.

fields.name: moderation
fields.description: Moderation level for the generated image. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: background
fields.description:
Background type for the generated image. One of `transparent`,
`opaque`, or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: input_image_mask
fields.description:
Optional mask for inpainting. Contains `image_url`
(string, optional) and `file_id` (string, optional).

fields.name: partial_images
fields.description: Number of partial images to generate in streaming mode, from 0 (default value) to 3.

types.description: A tool that generates images using the GPT image models.

types.name: ImageGenToolModelString
types.description: The image generation model to use. Default: `gpt-image-1`.

types.name: ImageGenToolQuality
types.description:
The quality of the generated image. One of `low`, `medium`, `high`,
or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

types.name: ImageGenToolSize
types.description: The size of the generated images. For `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-2026-04-21`, arbitrary resolutions are supported as `WIDTHxHEIGHT` strings, for example `1536x864`. Width and height must both be divisible by 16 and the requested aspect ratio must be between 1:3 and 3:1. Resolutions above `2560x1440` are experimental, and the maximum supported resolution is `3840x2160`. The requested size must also satisfy the model's current pixel and edge limits. The standard sizes `1024x1024`, `1536x1024`, and `1024x1536` are supported by the GPT image models; `auto` is supported for models that allow automatic sizing. For `dall-e-2`, use one of `256x256`, `512x512`, or `1024x1024`. For `dall-e-3`, use one of `1024x1024`, `1792x1024`, or `1024x1792`.

types.name: ImageGenToolOutputFormat
types.description:
The output format of the generated image. One of `png`, `webp`, or
`jpeg`. Default: `png`.

types.name: ImageGenToolModeration
types.description: Moderation level for the generated image. Default: `auto`.

types.name: ImageGenToolBackground
types.description:
Background type for the generated image. One of `transparent`,
`opaque`, or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

types.name: InputFidelity
types.description: Control how much effort the model will exert to match the style and features, especially facial features, of input images. This parameter is only supported for `gpt-image-1` and `gpt-image-1.5` and later models, unsupported for `gpt-image-1-mini`. Supports `high` and `low`. Defaults to `low`.

types.name: ImageGenToolInputImageMask

fields.name: image_url
fields.description: Base64-encoded mask image.

fields.name: file_id
fields.description: File ID for the mask image.

types.description:
Optional mask for inpainting. Contains `image_url`
(string, optional) and `file_id` (string, optional).

types.name: ApplyPatchToolCallOutput

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the item. Always `apply_patch_call_output`.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of the apply patch tool call output. Populated when this item is returned via API.

fields.name: call_id
fields.description: The unique ID of the apply patch tool call generated by the model.

fields.name: created_by
fields.description: The ID of the entity that created this tool call output.

types.description: The output emitted by an apply patch tool call.

types.name: McpApprovalResponseResource

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the item. Always `mcp_approval_response`.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of the approval response

fields.name: approval_request_id
fields.description: The ID of the approval request being answered.

fields.name: approve
fields.description: Whether the request was approved.

types.description: A response to an MCP approval request.

methods.description: Create items in a conversation with the given ID.

pathParams.name: conversation_id
pathParams.description: The ID of the conversation to add the item to.

queryParams.name: include
queryParams.description:
Additional fields to include in the response. See the `include`
parameter for [listing Conversation items above](/docs/api-reference/conversations/list-items#conversations_list_items-include) for more information.
